Hey guys, Saturday, time for your extra food for thought motivation. And again, these are clips that I'm going to do research for each week and find yourself. I think some of the really great moments where I was on stage, said things that really didn't have to do that much with selling, more about life in general, food for thought. Now if you're selling out there in the field today, it's Saturday, right? Hey, well, guess what? You know, make sure you go back and listen to a daily motivation from another day, right? Get yourself pumped up. But this is more just for you personally to kind of crystallize what success is about, what life is about, and how you can feel good about yourself. So check out this clip. You're going to enjoy it. And I'm not going to see you on the other side. It's going to go right to the ending. My gift to you. And you'll get one tomorrow as well. Ultimately, the mistake I made was very simple. I'll tell you what it was. The system was incredibly effective to the point where it's still today. It's still used all over Wall Street. Every big firm uses a straight line. If you look at my wall on Facebook, everyone with a straight line, this is the whole thing. I didn't even want to teach it. When I first started going, I taught everything but the straight line because I thought, oh, people don't commit bad things with it. It's like, pull out my own limiting belief. The system's beautiful and elegant. The problem was is that I, myself, at the age of 26, became greedy. And that's it. At the heart of my problems, I would like to say it was drugs that made me do things that I went to jail for 22 months because I manipulated some stocks. And I deserved to go to jail for that because I took this unbelievable, beautiful system that it works in any business and I bastardized it by allowing them to use it to convince people to do shit they shouldn't do. I made my average income was 50, 60 million a year in my 20s. I could have made 30 million legitimately. 95% of what I did was strictly legit. It was the 5% that took me off the reservation and I paid the ultimate price for it. The idea is that greed is not good. But the belief that Gordon Deco inserted in me, my ass, when I saw he's on stage, greed is good. Remember the movie, right? Greed is good. I was like, fuck, greed is good. I thought that was true. It's not. It's ambition. That's good. Greed doesn't cut through and clarify anything. Ambition cuts through. Ambition clarifies to have a vision for a better future, not just for you, for your family, for the people you love. That's what I was missing. So I took the system that I just made as much as I could, as quick as I could, and I paid the price. Ultimately, I'm fortunate. I wrote a book. I got lucky. I wrote this book and it got picked up all over the world. The cap year goes, oh, great, great, great, great, great. My point is this, okay, is that if you can look at my life in any way, I want you to be able to pick up the great stuff from it, and this stuff, which is amazing, but learn from the bad too, is that when you develop this power and you really, and you can do this stuff and really use it, only use it to help people get the things they need and to make decisions they should be making. Don't go off the reservation.
